source_evidence: Appendix B.6, Eq. (31)-(36): infinite cylinder axis n = B - A, radius r; quadratic 0 = a t_w^2 + b t_w + c with a = (n.n)(d.d) - (d.n)^2, b/2 = (n.n)(v.d) - (d.n)(v.n), c = (n.n)[(v.v) - r^2] - (v.n)^2; t_w = (-b/2 +- sqrt((b/2)^2 - ac))/a. Also 'segment completely contained' test Eq (36).
purpose: Tests whether a truss member (segment) intersects an infinite cylinder restriction primitive, either by crossing its surface (quadratic roots) or by being fully contained within it.
assumptions: Cylinder is of infinite length with axis n = B - A
Segment parameterized as X = P + td, 0 <= t <= 1; here v = P - A
Collision if segment crosses the surface OR is completely contained within the cylinder
formula_or_algorithm: d = Q - P, v = P - A, n = B - A. Solve quadratic a t_w^2 + b t_w + c = 0 with a = (n.n)(d.d) - (d.n)^2, b/2 = (n.n)(v.d) - (d.n)(v.n), c = (n.n)[(v.v) - r^2] - (v.n)^2. Roots t_w = (-b/2 +- sqrt((b/2)^2 - a c))/a. If discriminant < 0 the line misses the cylinder. If positive, a surface hit requires 0 <= t_w <= 1 for a root (points W, W'). Additionally the segment is fully contained if the distance from the axis to P satisfies (v - (v.n/n.n)n).(v - (v.n/n.n)n) <= r^2 (Eq. 36).
validation_opportunities: unit tests: chord piercing surface, tangent, fully inside, fully outside, parallel to axis
degenerate a = 0 (segment parallel to axis) handling

reuse_risk.explanation: Quadratic formulation is standard but numerically delicate: a=0 (parallel to axis) and near-tangent cases need special handling; contained-segment test must be combined with surface test.

missing_information: Handling when a = 0 (segment parallel to axis)
Combination logic between surface-hit and contained tests in code
